summary:
• Middle East geopolitical tensions and potential Iran conflict are driving Western Hemisphere producers like Nigeria to increase output, but broader shipping disruptions and potential Strait of Hormuz constraints threaten global supply route reliability.

• U.S. crude inventories are declining rapidly while refinery capacity expansions remain in development, creating a tightening domestic supply-demand balance that could pressure energy security if production disruptions occur.

• LNG supply shocks and conventional fuel shortages are creating downstream energy market volatility with spillover effects to regional markets, while alternative energy transitions (EVs, renewables) are gradually reducing oil demand growth in developed economies.
